hint: Sort intervals by start time. Then iterate and compare each interval with the last merged one. If they overlap (current.start <= lastMerged.end), extend the end. Otherwise, add a new interval.

title: Kth Largest Element
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element in the array. Note that it is the kth largest element in sorted order.
hint: Sort intervals by start time. Then iterate and compare each interval with the last merged one. If they overlap (current.start <= lastMerged.end), extend the end. Otherwise, add a new interval.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Find Peak Element
description: Given an integer array nums, find a peak element and return its index. A peak element is strictly greater than its neighbors. The array may contain multiple peaks.
hint: Use binary search. Compare nums[mid] with nums[mid+1]. If nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], the peak is in the right half (move left = mid+1). Otherwise, the peak is in the left half including mid.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Recursively swap the left and right children at every node. Base case: if the node is null, return null. The recursive call is: swap(node.left, node.right), then recurse on both children.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.
hint: Use a fixed-size sliding window of length k. Compute the sum of the first k elements, then slide by adding the next element and removing the leftmost. Track the maximum sum found.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of 1 bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

title: Course Schedule
description: There are n courses labeled 0 to n-1. Some courses have prerequisites. Determine if it is possible to finish all courses given the prerequisite pairs.
hint: Model this as a directed graph and check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm with in-degree tracking, or DFS with coloring). If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

title: Unique Paths
description: A robot is on the top-left corner of an m x n grid. It can only move right or down. How many possible unique paths are there to the bottom-right corner?
hint: Build a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the LCS length for text1[0..i] and text2[0..j]. If characters match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1, otherwise take max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence. A subsequence can skip characters but maintains relative order.
hint: This has overlapping subproblems — a hallmark of Dynamic Programming. Define dp[i] as the answer for step i. The recurrence is dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]. Build the table bottom-up.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array of integers nums and a target value, return the index of the target if found, or -1 if not present. The algorithm must run in O(log n) time.
hint: Determine which half is sorted by comparing nums[left] with nums[mid]. Then check if the target lies within the sorted half. If yes, search that half; otherwise search the other half.

title: Coin Change
description: Given an array coins and an amount, return the fewest number of coins needed to make up that amount. If that amount cannot be made up, return -1.
hint: Use a 1D DP array where dp[amount] = minimum coins needed. Initialize dp[0]=0 and all others to infinity. For each coin, update dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j-coin]) for j from coin to amount.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An anagram is a rearrangement of letters.
hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
description: Given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, return an array of their intersection. Each element must appear as many times as it shows in both arrays.
hint: Build a frequency map (character counts) for one input, then verify it against the other. Collections.Counter or a simple dictionary works perfectly.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
hint: Use the Dutch National Flag algorithm with three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap 0s to the front (low) and 2s to the back (high), leaving 1s in the middle. Single pass O(n).

title: Two Sum

title: Sort Colors

hint: Use the Dutch National Flag algorithm with three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap 0s to the front (low) and 2s to the back (high), leaving 1s in the middle. Single pass O(n).

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use three pointers: prev (starts null), current (starts at head), and next (temp). At each step: save next, point current.next to prev, move prev to current, move current to next.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hint: Use a 1D DP array where dp[amount] = minimum coins needed. Initialize dp[0]=0 and all others to infinity. For each coin, update dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j-coin]) for j from coin to amount.

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values, i.e., from left to right, level by level.
hint: Recursively swap the left and right children at every node. Base case: if the node is null, return null. The recursive call is: swap(node.left, node.right), then recurse on both children.

title: Power of Two

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of ones (land) and zeros (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hint: Use DFS or BFS with a visited HashMap mapping original nodes to their clones. For each node, create a clone, then recursively clone all unvisited neighbors.

title: Maximum Average Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value.
hint: Keep a window with two pointers. Use a dictionary to count character frequencies inside the window. When all required characters are present, try shrinking from the left to find the minimum valid window.

title: Sort Colors
description: Given an array nums with n objects colored red (0), white (1), and blue (2), sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent, in order 0 1 2.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

title: Unique Paths
description: A robot is on the top-left corner of an m x n grid. It can only move right or down. How many possible unique paths are there to the bottom-right corner?
hint: Define dp[i] = max money from first i houses. Recurrence: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). You either skip house i or rob it plus the best from two houses back.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a rotated sorted array and a target, return the index of the target or -1 if not found. The array was originally sorted in ascending order then rotated at an unknown pivot.
hint: Use binary search. Compare nums[mid] with nums[mid+1]. If nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], the peak is in the right half (move left = mid+1). Otherwise, the peak is in the left half including mid.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Use recursion (DFS). The depth of a node is 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)). Base case: a null node has depth 0. This naturally visits every node once in O(n).

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of daily temperatures, return an array where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait until a warmer temperature. If no future warmer day exists, set it to 0.

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: This has overlapping subproblems — a hallmark of Dynamic Programming. Define dp[i] as the answer for step i. The recurrence is dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]. Build the table bottom-up.

hint: Use a Hash Map (dictionary) to store values as keys while you iterate through the array. For each element, check if the complement (target - current) already exists in the map. This reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n).

title: Search Insert Position
description: Given a sorted array and a target value, return the index where it would be inserted in order. The array contains distinct values.
hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array of integers nums and a target value, return the index of the target if found, or -1 if not present. The algorithm must run in O(log n) time.
hint: Use binary search. Compare nums[mid] with nums[mid+1]. If nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], the peak is in the right half (move left = mid+1). Otherwise, the peak is in the left half including mid.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of daily temperatures, return an array where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait until a warmer temperature. If no future warmer day exists, set it to 0.
hint: Use a Stack. Push opening brackets onto it. When you encounter a closing bracket, check if the top of the stack matches. If the stack is empty at the end, the string is valid.

title: Sort Colors
description: Given an array nums with n objects colored red (0), white (1), and blue (2), sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent, in order 0 1 2.
hint: Sort intervals by start time. Then iterate and compare each interval with the last merged one. If they overlap (current.start <= lastMerged.end), extend the end. Otherwise, add a new interval.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hint: Create a 2D grid dp[m][n]. Each cell dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] since you can only arrive from above or from the left. Base case: first row and first column are all 1.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: This has overlapping subproblems — a hallmark of Dynamic Programming. Define dp[i] as the answer for step i. The recurrence is dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]. Build the table bottom-up.

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just the characters (, ), {, }, [ and ], determine if the input string is valid. Brackets must be closed in the correct order.
hint: Use a Stack. Push opening brackets onto it. When you encounter a closing bracket, check if the top of the stack matches. If the stack is empty at the end, the string is valid.

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. A permutation is a rearrangement of all characters.
hint: Use the Sliding Window technique: maintain two pointers (left and right) that define a window. Expand right to include new characters, and shrink left when you encounter a duplicate. Track characters in a Set or frequency array.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST. A valid BST has all left subtree nodes less than root and all right subtree nodes greater.
hint: Recursively swap the left and right children at every node. Base case: if the node is null, return null. The recursive call is: swap(node.left, node.right), then recurse on both children.

title: Find Peak Element
description: Given an integer array nums, find a peak element and return its index. A peak element is strictly greater than its neighbors. The array may contain multiple peaks.
hint: Classic Binary Search: maintain left and right pointers. Compute mid = (left+right)//2. If nums[mid] == target, return mid. If nums[mid] < target, search right half. Otherwise search left half.

hint: Use the Dutch National Flag algorithm with three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap 0s to the front (low) and 2s to the back (high), leaving 1s in the middle. Single pass O(n).

hint: Determine which half is sorted by comparing nums[left] with nums[mid]. Then check if the target lies within the sorted half. If yes, search that half; otherwise search the other half.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
hint: Use a Monotonic Stack (decreasing). Iterate through temperatures. For each day, pop all stack elements with lower temperatures and record the distance. Push the current day's index onto the stack.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of ones (land) and zeros (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hint: Iterate through each cell. When you find a '1', increment island count and run DFS/BFS to mark all connected '1's as visited (change them to '0'). This prevents counting the same island twice.

hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph. Each node contains a value and a list of its neighbors.
hint: Use DFS or BFS with a visited HashMap mapping original nodes to their clones. For each node, create a clone, then recursively clone all unvisited neighbors.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t is included in the window.
hint: Use the Sliding Window technique: maintain two pointers (left and right) that define a window. Expand right to include new characters, and shrink left when you encounter a duplicate. Track characters in a Set or frequency array.

title: Search Insert Position
description: Given a sorted array and a target value, return the index where it would be inserted in order. The array contains distinct values.
hint: Classic Binary Search: maintain left and right pointers. Compute mid = (left+right)//2. If nums[mid] == target, return mid. If nums[mid] < target, search right half. Otherwise search left half.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST. A valid BST has all left subtree nodes less than root and all right subtree nodes greater.
hint: Use DFS with min/max bounds. For each node, verify it falls within (min, max). Go left with updated max=node.val, go right with updated min=node.val. Start with (-infinity, +infinity).

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise. An anagram uses the exact same letters the same number of times.
hint: Use a Hash Map (dictionary) to store values as keys while you iterate through the array. For each element, check if the complement (target - current) already exists in the map. This reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n).

title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution.
hint: A Hash Set is ideal here. Insert elements one by one — if an element already exists in the set before insertion, you have your answer immediately.

title: Course Schedule
description: There are n courses labeled 0 to n-1. Some courses have prerequisites. Determine if it is possible to finish all courses given the prerequisite pairs.
hint: Use DFS or BFS with a visited HashMap mapping original nodes to their clones. For each node, create a clone, then recursively clone all unvisited neighbors.

title: Group Anagrams

hint: Use a Hash Map (dictionary) to store values as keys while you iterate through the array. For each element, check if the complement (target - current) already exists in the map. This reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n).

hint: Define dp[i] = max money from first i houses. Recurrence: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). You either skip house i or rob it plus the best from two houses back.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An anagram is a rearrangement of letters.
hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values, i.e., from left to right, level by level.
hint: Use DFS with min/max bounds. For each node, verify it falls within (min, max). Go left with updated max=node.val, go right with updated min=node.val. Start with (-infinity, +infinity).

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. A power of two has exactly one bit set in its binary representation.
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of 1 bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values, i.e., from left to right, level by level.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: XOR all elements together. Since a XOR a = 0 and a XOR 0 = a, all duplicates cancel out, leaving only the single unique number. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: Use a 1D DP array where dp[amount] = minimum coins needed. Initialize dp[0]=0 and all others to infinity. For each coin, update dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j-coin]) for j from coin to amount.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array

hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

hint: Define dp[i] = max money from first i houses. Recurrence: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). You either skip house i or rob it plus the best from two houses back.

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence. A subsequence can skip characters but maintains relative order.
hint: Use a 1D DP array where dp[amount] = minimum coins needed. Initialize dp[0]=0 and all others to infinity. For each coin, update dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j-coin]) for j from coin to amount.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. A power of two has exactly one bit set in its binary representation.
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.
hint: Use the Sliding Window technique: maintain two pointers (left and right) that define a window. Expand right to include new characters, and shrink left when you encounter a duplicate. Track characters in a Set or frequency array.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of ones (land) and zeros (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hint: Iterate through each cell. When you find a '1', increment island count and run DFS/BFS to mark all connected '1's as visited (change them to '0'). This prevents counting the same island twice.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Sort Colors
description: Given an array nums with n objects colored red (0), white (1), and blue (2), sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent, in order 0 1 2.
hint: Sort intervals by start time. Then iterate and compare each interval with the last merged one. If they overlap (current.start <= lastMerged.end), extend the end. Otherwise, add a new interval.

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values, i.e., from left to right, level by level.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a rotated sorted array and a target, return the index of the target or -1 if not found. The array was originally sorted in ascending order then rotated at an unknown pivot.
hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Use recursion (DFS). The depth of a node is 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)). Base case: a null node has depth 0. This naturally visits every node once in O(n).

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array of integers nums and a target value, return the index of the target if found, or -1 if not present. The algorithm must run in O(log n) time.
hint: Classic Binary Search: maintain left and right pointers. Compute mid = (left+right)//2. If nums[mid] == target, return mid. If nums[mid] < target, search right half. Otherwise search left half.

title: Coin Change
description: Given an array coins and an amount, return the fewest number of coins needed to make up that amount. If that amount cannot be made up, return -1.
hint: Build a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the LCS length for text1[0..i] and text2[0..j]. If characters match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1, otherwise take max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of daily temperatures, return an array where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait until a warmer temperature. If no future warmer day exists, set it to 0.
hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST. A valid BST has all left subtree nodes less than root and all right subtree nodes greater.
hint: Use DFS with min/max bounds. For each node, verify it falls within (min, max). Go left with updated max=node.val, go right with updated min=node.val. Start with (-infinity, +infinity).

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: XOR all elements together. Since a XOR a = 0 and a XOR 0 = a, all duplicates cancel out, leaving only the single unique number. O(n) time, O(1) space.

hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

description: Given a 2D grid map of ones (land) and zeros (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hint: Model this as a directed graph and check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm with in-degree tracking, or DFS with coloring). If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

title: Maximum Average Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value.
hint: Create a frequency map of s1. Slide a window of size len(s1) across s2, maintaining a frequency map of the window. Compare the two maps at each position.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of 1 bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

title: Contains Duplicate

hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

hint: Classic Binary Search: maintain left and right pointers. Compute mid = (left+right)//2. If nums[mid] == target, return mid. If nums[mid] < target, search right half. Otherwise search left half.

hint: Build a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the LCS length for text1[0..i] and text2[0..j]. If characters match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1, otherwise take max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An anagram is a rearrangement of letters.
hint: Use a Hash Map (dictionary) to store values as keys while you iterate through the array. For each element, check if the complement (target - current) already exists in the map. This reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n).

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph. Each node contains a value and a list of its neighbors.
hint: Model this as a directed graph and check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm with in-degree tracking, or DFS with coloring). If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

title: Course Schedule
description: There are n courses labeled 0 to n-1. Some courses have prerequisites. Determine if it is possible to finish all courses given the prerequisite pairs.
hint: Iterate through each cell. When you find a '1', increment island count and run DFS/BFS to mark all connected '1's as visited (change them to '0'). This prevents counting the same island twice.

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: This has overlapping subproblems — a hallmark of Dynamic Programming. Define dp[i] as the answer for step i. The recurrence is dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]. Build the table bottom-up.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: Create a 2D grid dp[m][n]. Each cell dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] since you can only arrive from above or from the left. Base case: first row and first column are all 1.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of 1 bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: Merge Intervals

hint: Keep a window with two pointers. Use a dictionary to count character frequencies inside the window. When all required characters are present, try shrinking from the left to find the minimum valid window.

title: Valid Parentheses

hint: Iterate through each cell. When you find a '1', increment island count and run DFS/BFS to mark all connected '1's as visited (change them to '0'). This prevents counting the same island twice.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hint: Build a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the LCS length for text1[0..i] and text2[0..j]. If characters match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1, otherwise take max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
hint: Use three pointers: prev (starts null), current (starts at head), and next (temp). At each step: save next, point current.next to prev, move prev to current, move current to next.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST. A valid BST has all left subtree nodes less than root and all right subtree nodes greater.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise. An anagram uses the exact same letters the same number of times.
hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
description: Given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, return an array of their intersection. Each element must appear as many times as it shows in both arrays.
hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values, i.e., from left to right, level by level.
hint: Recursively swap the left and right children at every node. Base case: if the node is null, return null. The recursive call is: swap(node.left, node.right), then recurse on both children.

title: Search Insert Position
description: Given a sorted array and a target value, return the index where it would be inserted in order. The array contains distinct values.
hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of ones (land) and zeros (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hint: Use DFS or BFS with a visited HashMap mapping original nodes to their clones. For each node, create a clone, then recursively clone all unvisited neighbors.

title: Maximum Average Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value.
hint: Use the Sliding Window technique: maintain two pointers (left and right) that define a window. Expand right to include new characters, and shrink left when you encounter a duplicate. Track characters in a Set or frequency array.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just the characters (, ), {, }, [ and ], determine if the input string is valid. Brackets must be closed in the correct order.
hint: Use a Stack. Push opening brackets onto it. When you encounter a closing bracket, check if the top of the stack matches. If the stack is empty at the end, the string is valid.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Use recursion (DFS). The depth of a node is 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)). Base case: a null node has depth 0. This naturally visits every node once in O(n).

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hint: This has overlapping subproblems — a hallmark of Dynamic Programming. Define dp[i] as the answer for step i. The recurrence is dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]. Build the table bottom-up.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use three pointers: prev (starts null), current (starts at head), and next (temp). At each step: save next, point current.next to prev, move prev to current, move current to next.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value.
hint: Create a frequency map of s1. Slide a window of size len(s1) across s2, maintaining a frequency map of the window. Compare the two maps at each position.

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: Binary Search

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters

title: Number of Islands

title: Longest Common Subsequence

title: Search Insert Position

title: Power of Two

title: Reverse Linked List

title: Reverse Linked List

hint: Use a Monotonic Stack (decreasing). Iterate through temperatures. For each day, pop all stack elements with lower temperatures and record the distance. Push the current day's index onto the stack.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. A power of two has exactly one bit set in its binary representation.
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.
hint: Create a frequency map of s1. Slide a window of size len(s1) across s2, maintaining a frequency map of the window. Compare the two maps at each position.

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
description: Given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, return an array of their intersection. Each element must appear as many times as it shows in both arrays.
hint: Sort each string's characters to create a canonical key, then group strings sharing the same key using a Hash Map of lists.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST. A valid BST has all left subtree nodes less than root and all right subtree nodes greater.
hint: Recursively swap the left and right children at every node. Base case: if the node is null, return null. The recursive call is: swap(node.left, node.right), then recurse on both children.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Find Peak Element
description: Given an integer array nums, find a peak element and return its index. A peak element is strictly greater than its neighbors. The array may contain multiple peaks.
hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

title: Min Stack

hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

hint: Use the Dutch National Flag algorithm with three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap 0s to the front (low) and 2s to the back (high), leaving 1s in the middle. Single pass O(n).

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of 1 bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: Create a 2D grid dp[m][n]. Each cell dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] since you can only arrive from above or from the left. Base case: first row and first column are all 1.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return false if every element is distinct.
hint: Sort each string's characters to create a canonical key, then group strings sharing the same key using a Hash Map of lists.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
hint: Use a Monotonic Stack (decreasing). Iterate through temperatures. For each day, pop all stack elements with lower temperatures and record the distance. Push the current day's index onto the stack.

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: XOR all elements together. Since a XOR a = 0 and a XOR 0 = a, all duplicates cancel out, leaving only the single unique number. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.
hint: Use a fixed-size sliding window of length k. Compute the sum of the first k elements, then slide by adding the next element and removing the leftmost. Track the maximum sum found.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array of integers nums and a target value, return the index of the target if found, or -1 if not present. The algorithm must run in O(log n) time.
hint: Determine which half is sorted by comparing nums[left] with nums[mid]. Then check if the target lies within the sorted half. If yes, search that half; otherwise search the other half.

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. A permutation is a rearrangement of all characters.
hint: Use a fixed-size sliding window of length k. Compute the sum of the first k elements, then slide by adding the next element and removing the leftmost. Track the maximum sum found.

title: Unique Paths
description: A robot is on the top-left corner of an m x n grid. It can only move right or down. How many possible unique paths are there to the bottom-right corner?
hint: Use a 1D DP array where dp[amount] = minimum coins needed. Initialize dp[0]=0 and all others to infinity. For each coin, update dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j-coin]) for j from coin to amount.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Reverse Linked List

hint: Use recursion (DFS). The depth of a node is 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)). Base case: a null node has depth 0. This naturally visits every node once in O(n).

hint: Create a frequency map of s1. Slide a window of size len(s1) across s2, maintaining a frequency map of the window. Compare the two maps at each position.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of 1 bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hint: XOR all elements together. Since a XOR a = 0 and a XOR 0 = a, all duplicates cancel out, leaving only the single unique number. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hint: Use a 1D DP array where dp[amount] = minimum coins needed. Initialize dp[0]=0 and all others to infinity. For each coin, update dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j-coin]) for j from coin to amount.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return false if every element is distinct.
hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a rotated sorted array and a target, return the index of the target or -1 if not found. The array was originally sorted in ascending order then rotated at an unknown pivot.
hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST. A valid BST has all left subtree nodes less than root and all right subtree nodes greater.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of daily temperatures, return an array where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait until a warmer temperature. If no future warmer day exists, set it to 0.
hint: Use a Stack. Push opening brackets onto it. When you encounter a closing bracket, check if the top of the stack matches. If the stack is empty at the end, the string is valid.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array of integers nums and a target value, return the index of the target if found, or -1 if not present. The algorithm must run in O(log n) time.
hint: Classic Binary Search: maintain left and right pointers. Compute mid = (left+right)//2. If nums[mid] == target, return mid. If nums[mid] < target, search right half. Otherwise search left half.

title: Unique Paths
description: A robot is on the top-left corner of an m x n grid. It can only move right or down. How many possible unique paths are there to the bottom-right corner?
hint: Use a 1D DP array where dp[amount] = minimum coins needed. Initialize dp[0]=0 and all others to infinity. For each coin, update dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j-coin]) for j from coin to amount.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. A power of two has exactly one bit set in its binary representation.
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise. An anagram uses the exact same letters the same number of times.
hint: Use a Hash Map (dictionary) to store values as keys while you iterate through the array. For each element, check if the complement (target - current) already exists in the map. This reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n).

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of daily temperatures, return an array where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait until a warmer temperature. If no future warmer day exists, set it to 0.
hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: Build a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the LCS length for text1[0..i] and text2[0..j]. If characters match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1, otherwise take max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. A permutation is a rearrangement of all characters.
hint: Create a frequency map of s1. Slide a window of size len(s1) across s2, maintaining a frequency map of the window. Compare the two maps at each position.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An anagram is a rearrangement of letters.
hint: Sort each string's characters to create a canonical key, then group strings sharing the same key using a Hash Map of lists.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: Kth Largest Element
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element in the array. Note that it is the kth largest element in sorted order.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a rotated sorted array and a target, return the index of the target or -1 if not found. The array was originally sorted in ascending order then rotated at an unknown pivot.
hint: Use binary search. Compare nums[mid] with nums[mid+1]. If nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], the peak is in the right half (move left = mid+1). Otherwise, the peak is in the left half including mid.

title: Invert Binary Tree

hint: Recursively swap the left and right children at every node. Base case: if the node is null, return null. The recursive call is: swap(node.left, node.right), then recurse on both children.

hint: Use Floyd's Cycle Detection (tortoise and hare): slow pointer moves 1 step, fast pointer moves 2 steps. If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there is no cycle.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph. Each node contains a value and a list of its neighbors.
hint: Use DFS or BFS with a visited HashMap mapping original nodes to their clones. For each node, create a clone, then recursively clone all unvisited neighbors.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph. Each node contains a value and a list of its neighbors.
hint: Model this as a directed graph and check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm with in-degree tracking, or DFS with coloring). If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

title: Search Insert Position
description: Given a sorted array and a target value, return the index where it would be inserted in order. The array contains distinct values.
hint: Determine which half is sorted by comparing nums[left] with nums[mid]. Then check if the target lies within the sorted half. If yes, search that half; otherwise search the other half.

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. A permutation is a rearrangement of all characters.
hint: Keep a window with two pointers. Use a dictionary to count character frequencies inside the window. When all required characters are present, try shrinking from the left to find the minimum valid window.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST. A valid BST has all left subtree nodes less than root and all right subtree nodes greater.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree and return its root. Inverting means swapping every left child with the right child.
hint: Use DFS with min/max bounds. For each node, verify it falls within (min, max). Go left with updated max=node.val, go right with updated min=node.val. Start with (-infinity, +infinity).

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of daily temperatures, return an array where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait until a warmer temperature. If no future warmer day exists, set it to 0.
hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. A power of two has exactly one bit set in its binary representation.
hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise. An anagram uses the exact same letters the same number of times.
hint: Build a frequency map (character counts) for one input, then verify it against the other. Collections.Counter or a simple dictionary works perfectly.

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. A permutation is a rearrangement of all characters.
hint: Create a frequency map of s1. Slide a window of size len(s1) across s2, maintaining a frequency map of the window. Compare the two maps at each position.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An anagram is a rearrangement of letters.
hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Course Schedule
description: There are n courses labeled 0 to n-1. Some courses have prerequisites. Determine if it is possible to finish all courses given the prerequisite pairs.
hint: Iterate through each cell. When you find a '1', increment island count and run DFS/BFS to mark all connected '1's as visited (change them to '0'). This prevents counting the same island twice.

hint: Use the Dutch National Flag algorithm with three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap 0s to the front (low) and 2s to the back (high), leaving 1s in the middle. Single pass O(n).

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of ones (land) and zeros (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hint: Model this as a directed graph and check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm with in-degree tracking, or DFS with coloring). If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise. An anagram uses the exact same letters the same number of times.
hint: Use a Hash Map (dictionary) to store values as keys while you iterate through the array. For each element, check if the complement (target - current) already exists in the map. This reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n).

title: Kth Largest Element

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of 1 bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t is included in the window.
hint: Keep a window with two pointers. Use a dictionary to count character frequencies inside the window. When all required characters are present, try shrinking from the left to find the minimum valid window.

title: Clone Graph

title: Kth Largest Element

title: Maximum Average Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value.

title: Contains Duplicate

title: Power of Two

hint: Model this as a directed graph and check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm with in-degree tracking, or DFS with coloring). If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values, i.e., from left to right, level by level.
hint: Use Breadth-First Search with a Queue. Start with the root. For each level, process all nodes currently in the queue, adding their children for the next level. Collect values per level.

title: Find Peak Element
description: Given an integer array nums, find a peak element and return its index. A peak element is strictly greater than its neighbors. The array may contain multiple peaks.
hint: Use binary search but instead of returning -1 when not found, return the left pointer after the loop ends — that is exactly where the target should be inserted to maintain sorted order.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just the characters (, ), {, }, [ and ], determine if the input string is valid. Brackets must be closed in the correct order.
hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. A power of two has exactly one bit set in its binary representation.
hint: XOR all elements together. Since a XOR a = 0 and a XOR 0 = a, all duplicates cancel out, leaving only the single unique number. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t is included in the window.
hint: Keep a window with two pointers. Use a dictionary to count character frequencies inside the window. When all required characters are present, try shrinking from the left to find the minimum valid window.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t is included in the window.
hint: Keep a window with two pointers. Use a dictionary to count character frequencies inside the window. When all required characters are present, try shrinking from the left to find the minimum valid window.

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. A permutation is a rearrangement of all characters.
hint: Use the Sliding Window technique: maintain two pointers (left and right) that define a window. Expand right to include new characters, and shrink left when you encounter a duplicate. Track characters in a Set or frequency array.

title: Find Peak Element
description: Given an integer array nums, find a peak element and return its index. A peak element is strictly greater than its neighbors. The array may contain multiple peaks.
hint: Determine which half is sorted by comparing nums[left] with nums[mid]. Then check if the target lies within the sorted half. If yes, search that half; otherwise search the other half.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.
hint: Keep a window with two pointers. Use a dictionary to count character frequencies inside the window. When all required characters are present, try shrinking from the left to find the minimum valid window.

title: Sort Colors
description: Given an array nums with n objects colored red (0), white (1), and blue (2), sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent, in order 0 1 2.
hint: Use a Min-Heap of size k. Iterate through all elements, pushing each one. If heap size exceeds k, pop the smallest. At the end, the heap's top is the kth largest. O(n log k) time.

title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution.
hint: Sort each string's characters to create a canonical key, then group strings sharing the same key using a Hash Map of lists.

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence. A subsequence can skip characters but maintains relative order.
hint: Build a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the LCS length for text1[0..i] and text2[0..j]. If characters match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1, otherwise take max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: XOR all elements together. Since a XOR a = 0 and a XOR 0 = a, all duplicates cancel out, leaving only the single unique number. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph. Each node contains a value and a list of its neighbors.
hint: Model this as a directed graph and check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm with in-degree tracking, or DFS with coloring). If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return false if every element is distinct.
hint: Store elements of the smaller array in a Hash Set, then iterate through the larger array checking for membership. This gives O(n+m) time complexity.

title: Maximum Average Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value.
hint: Use a fixed-size sliding window of length k. Compute the sum of the first k elements, then slide by adding the next element and removing the leftmost. Track the maximum sum found.

hint: Sort intervals by start time. Then iterate and compare each interval with the last merged one. If they overlap (current.start <= lastMerged.end), extend the end. Otherwise, add a new interval.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge the two sorted linked lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hint: Use a dummy head node. Compare the values at the front of both lists. Append the smaller one to the result and advance that list's pointer. Continue until one list is empty, then append the remainder.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
hint: Maintain two stacks: one for actual values and one for tracking the current minimum. When pushing, also push onto the min stack if the value is <= current min. When popping, pop from min stack if the value equals current min.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hint: Use three pointers: prev (starts null), current (starts at head), and next (temp). At each step: save next, point current.next to prev, move prev to current, move current to next.

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence. A subsequence can skip characters but maintains relative order.
hint: Define dp[i] = max money from first i houses. Recurrence: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). You either skip house i or rob it plus the best from two houses back.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree and return its root. Inverting means swapping every left child with the right child.
hint: Recursively swap the left and right children at every node. Base case: if the node is null, return null. The recursive call is: swap(node.left, node.right), then recurse on both children.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hint: Create a 2D grid dp[m][n]. Each cell dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] since you can only arrive from above or from the left. Base case: first row and first column are all 1.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. A power of two has exactly one bit set in its binary representation.
hint: XOR all elements together. Since a XOR a = 0 and a XOR 0 = a, all duplicates cancel out, leaving only the single unique number. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Kth Largest Element
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element in the array. Note that it is the kth largest element in sorted order.
hint: Use the Dutch National Flag algorithm with three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap 0s to the front (low) and 2s to the back (high), leaving 1s in the middle. Single pass O(n).

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find the single element. Your solution should have linear runtime and use no extra memory.
hint: A power of two in binary has exactly one 1-bit. Use the trick: n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0. The expression n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit — if that makes it zero, it was a power of 2.

hint: Use three pointers: prev (starts null), current (starts at head), and next (temp). At each step: save next, point current.next to prev, move prev to current, move current to next.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Use DFS with min/max bounds. For each node, verify it falls within (min, max). Go left with updated max=node.val, go right with updated min=node.val. Start with (-infinity, +infinity).

title: House Robber
description: Given an integer array representing the amount of money at each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
hint: Create a 2D grid dp[m][n]. Each cell dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] since you can only arrive from above or from the left. Base case: first row and first column are all 1.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of ones (land) and zeros (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hint: Use DFS or BFS with a visited HashMap mapping original nodes to their clones. For each node, create a clone, then recursively clone all unvisited neighbors.

hint: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit. Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift n repeatedly.

hint: Sort intervals by start time. Then iterate and compare each interval with the last merged one. If they overlap (current.start <= lastMerged.end), extend the end. Otherwise, add a new interval.

hint: Use a Hash Map (dictionary) to store values as keys while you iterate through the array. For each element, check if the complement (target - current) already exists in the map. This reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n).
